intro: Radamaea perrieri, also known as the Madagascar radamia, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its unique succulent leaves and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. Bioactive constituents of Radamaea perrieri include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Malagasy medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused in water to create herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
